Suzuki Museum, 99% of Japan's gold leaf; getting there ex-SIN (Hokur
destinations.next_eligible: ~Dec 2026 — only with a genuine new angle (a new season, a major opening, or the Noto recovery as its own story).

destinations.next_eligible: ~Dec 2026 — only with a genuine new angle (a new season, a major opening, or a NZ-summer hiking trip on the new non-stop CHC route).

destinations.next_eligible: ~Jan 2027 — only with a genuine new angle (a cooler-season return, a new route, or a specialist lens such as a pure camera/audio crawl).

destinations.issue: Another "Japan beyond the Golden Route" pick with a family-walkable spine: Matsumoto's original black castle, the preserved Nakasendo post towns (the Tsumago–Magome walk), the Japan Alps backdrop, soba and craft; Shinkansen + local-rail reachable, photogenic, calmer than Kyoto. Castle + history + alpine photography + an 11-year-old's walk.
destinations.angle: r/JapanTravel, TripAdvisor Matsumoto/Kiso, Tabelog, hiking/photo blogs
destinations.next_eligible: Autumn foliage on the Nakasendo; spring; clear-air Alps in winter.

destinations.destination: Hokkaido (Niseko + the east), Japan** — COVERED, No. 39 (Weekend Travel Desk, 11 Jul 2026)
destinations.issue: "The fields are at their best right now" — timed to Furano/Biei's tightest lavender bloom window (10–25 Jul); the Niseko-summer/Whistler-winter ski parallel as a secondary thread; eastern Hokkaido's dark skies (Lake Mashu, Rikubetsu) flagged honestly as a winter-better, July-secondary astro thread; honesty flags on SIA's winter-only direct route and the Blue Pond's earlier true peak.
destinations.angle: r/skiing, Niseko/Snow-Forecast forums, r/JapanTravel, Cloudy Nights / AstroBin (dark-sky sites); TripAdvisor Hokkaido Forum
destinations.next_eligible: ~Jan 2027 — only with a genuine new angle (the ski/powder season itself, a new Niseko hotel opening e.g. Aman Niseko or Hotel101, or the eastern dark-sky thread run properly in winter).

destinations.issue: Benesse/Chichu art and Tadao Ando architecture; ferry-hopping; a quieter, photogenic Japan that's family-doable; Triennale years add a peg. Strong art-and-photography fit.
destinations.angle: r/JapanTravel, TripAdvisor Naoshima/Setouchi, Setouchi Triennale official, photo blogs
destinations.next_eligible: Mild spring/autumn; Triennale season when on.

destinations.issue: Dark skies + aurora australis; MONA (provocative art museum); wilderness photography; food/whisky; family-friendly nature; reachable via an AU hop. Astro + art + photography.
destinations.angle: r/tasmania, r/astrophotography (aurora alerts), TripAdvisor, MONA/Dark Mofo
destinations.next_eligible: Austral winter for aurora; Dark Mofo (Jun).

destinations.destination: Switzerland — the watch valleys \& the Alps
destinations.issue: A watchmaking pilgrimage (Vallée de Joux, manufacture/museum visits) — the premium/horology fit; Jungfrau/Zermatt alpine photography; Alpine dark skies; family rail. The aspirational "big trip."
destinations.angle: r/Switzerland, Watchuseek/r/Watches travel threads, FlyerTalk (premium cabin to ZRH/GVA), TripAdvisor
destinations.next_eligible: Summer alpine or shoulder; book manufacture visits ahead.

destinations.issue: Aurora + landscape photography (a photographer's destination); family ring-road; geothermal. The big northern trip; pairs with the astro thread.
destinations.next_eligible: Aurora season (autumn–spring) vs midnight-sun summer — flag the trade-off.

destinations.issue: The planet's best dark skies — ALMA/observatory tours, bucket-list astrophotography, otherworldly landscapes. Flag as long-haul/big-trip; a "someday" anchor for the astro thread.
destinations.angle: r/astrophotography, r/chile, TripAdvisor San Pedro de Atacama, observatory booking sites

destinations.issue: Food, watch/camera/audio retail, skyline photography, very family-doable, short-haul. A dense, high-energy weekend-plus pick.

Short-ish-haul; camera/audio & watch retail (Yongsan, vintage); food and night markets; Bukhansan day-hikes; very family-friendly; a tech-forward city that mirrors the reader's interests. High value-per-flight-hour, all-season.
destinations.angle: r/korea, r/seoul, TripAdvisor, Naver/Mangoplate reviews, FlyerTalk
destinations.next_eligible: Spring blossom / autumn foliage; clear-air winter.

destinations.destination: Aoraki / Mt Cook & the Catlins (South Island, NZ — summer)
destinations.issue: The NZ-summer counterpart now that the non-stop Christchurch service opens it: alpine hiking, the Hooker Valley, the Catlins' dark coast & waterfalls; a future warm-season return that uses the new route honestly (no ski, long days).
destinations.angle: r/newzealand, TripAdvisor, DOC trail notes, r/hiking
destinations.next_eligible: NZ summer (Dec–Mar) on the new non-stop CHC route.

destinations.issue: Very short-haul (~1h20 ex-SIN), family-easy and cheap; a UNESCO heritage core for street photography (the murals, the clan jetties, colonial shophouses); one of Asia's great hawker-food cities; Peranakan craft & antiques. Hits food + photography + an 11-year-old's ease at high value-per-flight-hour, no jet lag.
destinations.angle: r/malaysia, r/Penang, TripAdvisor George Town, Google/Foursquare hawker reviews
destinations.next_eligible: Drier Dec–Feb; George Town Festival (usually mid-year); avoid the wettest Sep–Nov.

destinations.issue: Short-haul; crafts, temples and night bazaars; strong coffee/food scene; elephant-sanctuary & moat-city family logistics; the Yi Peng/Loy Krathong lantern release as a signature photographic peg; walkable and inexpensive. Food + photography + family fit.
destinations.angle: r/Thailand, r/ThailandTourism, TripAdvisor Chiang Mai, Google/Wongnai reviews
destinations.next_eligible: Cool-dry Nov–Feb; Yi Peng (Nov); avoid the Mar–Apr burning season.
